name: Sausage and Pork Jambalaya
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown the pork in a large pot with a small amount of water on the bottom to prevent sticking. Cover and stir occasionally.
2. Add onions and continue to sauté until transparent.
3. When onions are transparent, add sausage and continue to sauté.
4. When meat is cooked, add brown gravy and cook for a few more minutes.
5. Add an equal amount of water as rice (5 cups in this case). Allow the water to heat, then turn off the heat and cover.
6. Skim off the grease with a large spoon. Conserve the grease.
7. After skimming, add an amount of water equal to the amount of grease to replace lost liquid.
8. Bring to almost boiling and add seasonings (salt, cayenne pepper, Tiger sauce, hot sauce, Tony's Chachere's Seasoning). Heat spices briefly and taste. Adjust seasoning as desired.
9. Add shallots when ready to finish cooking.
10. Optional: Add a small amount of Kitchen Bouquet for color.
11. Bring to rolling boil and add rice all at once. Stir until milky with the rice.
12. Reduce heat slightly, cover, and let steam occasionally. Open lid sparingly and wipe before replacing.
13. As liquid dries up, reduce heat further and allow rice to finish steaming, stirring every few minutes and scraping bottom.
14. Jambalaya is done when rice is tender.
